Request More Info tierweb Nationally Ranked Best 250 Graduate Schools by Abound - 2024 and 2023 25 Most Affordable Colleges for an Online Masterâs Degree (#13) by Online Master's Degrees - 2023 Best Graduate Schools by Abound - 2022 Best Master's Degrees in Missouri by University HQ - 2022 Top Grad School Programs by Abound - 2021 Graduate Admission Requirements To apply for admission to a masterâs degree program at UCM you must have a bachelorâs degree. To apply to an Education Specialistâs degree program you must have a masterâs degree. Prerequisite degrees must be from institutions accredited by agencies recognized by the University of Central Missouri. If you have graduate coursework you must have a graduate GPA of 3.0 or higher to be admitted to a graduate program. Department Admission Requirements Some graduate programs at UCM require standardized tests, such as the GRE or GMAT, and some require a minimum GPA, letters of recommendation, a statement of purpose, and/or other materials. To learn more about the requirements for your specific program, please review the Graduate Catalog. For specific program questions, please contact the designated graduate coordinator for your area of study. Review our application instructions . Review our application material submission instructions . Mule Express Through âMule Express,â a student can expect to reap a range of benefits that will not only put cash back in their pockets but help make their transition into a graduate program as simple as possible. The âMule Expressâ program offers the following to help accommodate UCM alumni pursuing a masterâs degree: No application fee : UCM is waiving the application fee for all university alumni and currently enrolled undergraduate students in an effort to make earning a graduate degree intellectually and financially rewarding. Transcript simplification : UCM students need not worry about resubmitting transcripts they have already provided during their time here, unless they have attended another institution since graduating from UCM. This streamlined approach recognizes a university alumnusâ prior commitment to UCM and saves them time. Expedited application review : UCM understands just how valuable time is for our alumni. With "Mule Express," applications from alumni and current students will receive expedited reviews, ensuring that they can swiftly move forward with their educational aspirations. âMule Expressâ applies to domestic UCM students applying for a graduate certificate program, a masterâs degree, or an Educational Specialist degree. Individuals who want to apply for admission or simply learn more about the âMule Expressâ initiative are encouraged to contact UCM Graduate Studies at gradadmit@ucmo.edu . Have Questions? We Are Here to Help If you have questions about applying to UCM, please contact the Office of Graduate Studies. Email gradadmit@ucmo.edu , call 660-543-4729 . International Admissions at the University of Central Missouri Banner International Student Services The World is at UCM! The University of Central Missouri is truly a global experience with more than 798 international students representing 49 countries . Located in Warrensburg, Missouri, UCM provides the perfect balance of small-town-America charm and safety, affordable prices, academic excellence, high employment rates, and urban excitement. We offer 150+ nationally and globally competitive programs accredited by the Higher Learning Commission . Our students enjoy an extremely high success rate â 94% of UCM students launch their career or continue their education within 6 months of graduation . Our exceptional student support ensures that you are understood and empowered to achieve your goals. Your Safety is Important to Us. Safety is a big concern of yours â and ours. UCM is protected 24/7 by its very own Department of Public Safety that includes 19 uniformed officers and operates like any police department in the United States. Plus, our SAFE ( Student Assistant Foot patrol and Escort ) Team patrols the campus at night to help prevent crime and offers walking escorts. UCM also provides free self-defense programs taught by Public Safety officers. For increased security, all residential buildings with exterior door access have electronic locks . UCM students are automatically enrolled in UCMAlerts powered by Everbridge. Verify your contact information is correct in MyCentral to receive emergency alerts and other important notifications via text and email. You can download the optional Everbridge 360 app by clicking here or visiting the Apple App Store or Google Play. International admissions requirements vary depending upon your student type and whether you are pursuing an undergraduate or graduate degree. However, all international students will be required to: Complete the International Student Application Pay the non-refundable $75 application fee* Prove English proficiency* Provide financial support letter Provide a bank statement with adequate funds Official Transcripts * Photocopy of passport * may not apply to all student types Click Here for full admission requirements International Student Services Appeals Disputed Fee Payment and Test Score Cancellation Prospective University of Central Missouri (UCM) graduate students denied admission due to disputed fee payment or test score cancellation may petition to reinstate UCM admission, or to reinstate the opportunity to apply for admission at UCM. In the event of an appeal approval for disputed fee payments, the prospective student must successfully repay the application fee. In the event of an appeal approval for test score cancellation, the prospective student must successfully submit an updated test score, or be eligible for a test score waiver under existing waiver policies at UCM. In addition to submitting the below appeals form, the request must include: explanation/justification for this appeal documentation of the circumstances surrounding the incident, if applicable detailed plan of how you will successfully meet UCM admission requirements in relation to fee payment and/or test score submission moving forward Appeals Form Have Questions? Email intladmit@ucmo.edu , call 001.660.543.4092 or participate in a live chat. In order to fulfill this mission, the Office of the Registrar & Student Records: Provides secure and confidential maintenance of student academic records in perpetuity. Complies with the mandates of the Family Educational Rights and Privacy Act (FERPA). Interprets, implements, ensures compliance, and communicates changes regarding academic policies and procedures. Assesses student progression toward timely completion of educational goals. Supports a positive work environment based on integrity, service, and inclusion. Values Statement The Office of the Registrar and Student Records supports and abides by the UCM Ethics Policy and Nondiscrimination/Equal Opportunity Statement as set forth by the Board of Governors. We also adhere to the best practices and ethical standards adopted by the American Association of Collegiate Registrars and Admissions Officers (AACRAO) and to the Professional Standards for Higher Education as outlined by the Council for the Advancement of Standards in Higher Education (CAS) . There are two types of academic transcripts â unofficial and official. Currently enrolled students may access unofficial transcripts in MyCentral. National Student Clearinghouse E-Transcripts and Mailed Transcript Ordering through National Student Clearinghouse To order an official transcript using National Student Clearinghouse, you will be creating an account that will allow: Online Ordering - Request Secure Documents quickly and easily Secure Payment - Pay for your order online using a secure credit card checkout Order History - View the history of your requests that have been made through National Student Clearinghouse MyCentral If you are a currently enrolled UCM student and know your MyCentral login information you can proceed to MyCentral. Select "Discover More" and search for "Transcript". From here, you can select either Official or Unofficial transcript. Need a faxed transcript? Some institutions/companies will accept faxed transcripts. Before placing an order for a faxed transcript, confirm that the receiving institution will accept this format as official. Transcripts should be faxed directly to the receiving institution. Make sure you have supplied the correct fax number. Students and former students can order a faxed transcript using our Transcript Request Form . Transcripts are faxed within 48 business hours from the UCM Registrar's Office (fax: 660-543-8400). Official Transcripts Official Transcripts are provided for a fee ($10 each) by the Office of the Registrar and Student Records. Paper, faxed, and electronic transcripts are available. Prior to placing an order for a faxed or electronic transcript, please check with the receiving institution to confirm that they will accept these methods as official. Official transcripts include your name, your birth month and day (not year), and your UCM student number. For your privacy your social security number is not included on the official transcript. If you have completed a degree at UCM, the transcript will include your degree(s), major(s), and minor(s) (if applicable). If you graduated with honors this will also be displayed on your transcript. Dean's List designation will show with applicable semesters. Transcripts for Dual Credit Students who earned dual credit through UCM during high school (or who participated in Boys or Girls State) may order official transcripts through the National Student Clearinghouse. The Registrarâs Office cannot accept transcript requests by telephone. Transcripts for Dale Carnegie Credit Dale CarnegieÂ® programs are in compliance with the rigorous standards of the American Council on Education (ACE) and are considered to be of college level quality. ACE recommends to colleges and universities throughout the U.S. that individuals completing Dale CarnegieÂ® Training as of April 28, 1983 are eligible for college transfer credit. When transferring credits to another college or university, the final decision whether or not transfer credits will be awarded for completing a Dale CarnegieÂ® Training program is determined by the receiving institution. Please contact the college or university that you are currently attending, or plan to attend in the future, to determine the policies and procedures regarding transfer credits. Some institutions will not use these credits as general education, major, or minor coursework, but may be accepted as elective credits. Students who have completed Dale Carnegie credits at UCM should follow the regular process for ordering an official UCM transcript (see above). Students who completed Dale CarnegieÂ® training through other locations (not through UCM) should use the Dale Carnegie Transcript Request form and follow the instructions below. The certificate programs that UCM will accept can be found listed on the request form. After completing your Dale CarnegieÂ® Training program, please submit the following items to your local Dale CarnegieÂ® Representative: A completed University of Central Missouri Dale Carnegie Transcript Request Form. A check or money order made payable to the University of Central Missouri for $50.00 for the first transcript request and $10.00 for each additional transcript request. A copy of your Certificate of Completion from your Dale CarnegieÂ® Training Program. Your local Dale CarnegieÂ® representative will verify that you have successfully completed your Dale CarnegieÂ® Training program and will submit your forms to the University of Central Missouri for processing. The course will appear as transfer work on the transcript. Upon UCM's receipt of request, please allow a minimum of two weeks for processing your transcript(s). Frequently Asked Questions May I request a transcript over the phone? No, in order to ensure confidentiality requests cannot be taken by phone. Written consent is required to release a transcript. Written consent is: Physical signature if using the Transcript Request Form (PDF) Electronic signature if ordering through National Student Clearinghouse May transcript requests be faxed? Yes, requests may be faxed to 660-543-8400. Please print and fill out our transcript request form . Faxed requests require credit/debit card payment, please call the Registrar's Office to make the payment 660-543-4900. Faxed and e-mailed transcripts may not be considered official by the receiving institution/party. Please check with the receiving institution before placing your transcript order. Transcript fees are non-refundable. Transcripts cannot be faxed overseas. I am a student at another school, but I took a course at UCM this semester. How do I get my grade sent back to my school? Students may obtain an official copy of their University of Central Missouri transcript in the Office of the Registrar by showing your photo ID. See instructions above to request a transcript online, by mail, e-mail, or by fax. Is a copy of my high school transcript or other college transcripts on file in the Registrar's Office? No. The UCM Registrar maintains only UCM academic records. It is the student's responsibility to obtain transcripts from previously attended institutions. I took dual credit classes through UCM and want to transfer those credits to another university. What do I do? Students who earned dual credit through UCM during high school may request an official UCM transcript through National Student Clearinghouse. The Registrarâs Office cannot accept transcript requests by telephone. Follow the instructions above on this page for more information on ordering an official transcript. I donât need an official copy of my transcript, but could just use an unofficial one. How do I get an unofficial copy? The Registrar's Office does not issue unofficial transcripts . Currently enrolled students can access their unofficial transcript by logging into MyCentral. Once logged in, select the Students/Academics tab and select the "Unofficial Transcript" card. Coursework information is the same on both unofficial and official transcripts. Unofficial transcripts do not indicate whether a degree has been awarded. Degrees earned are posted only to official transcripts. Dean's List designation will show with applicable semesters. social-section Â©

Two of the most important documents to students are their UCM academic transcript (either official or unofficial) and the Central Degree Audit. Our goal is to ensure UCM students, faculty and staff have the appropriate technology resources to advance the University's mission, vision and values. The Office of Technology at the University of Central Missouri has the responsibility and duties to leverage and support technology resources for academic activities and administrative functions across campus that directly and indirectly support student success, including: Campus workstations (desktops, laptops, tablets, mobile devices, etc.) Enterprise and departmental applications Data and voice network infrastructure, servers Storage arrays, network connectivity, identity management Technology in higher education is ever-changing. The Office of Technology navigates the fine balance which exists between cutting-edge technology and the ability to provide stable and robust technology resources deployed, leveraged, and maintained by the University. New or returning students can Get Started with the Student Technology Guide - an overview of the technology resources available to all students. Technology Services and Account Management Technology touches every part of higher education. The Office of Technology collaborates with the academic departments and offices to ensure the University of Central Missouri is prepared to face and adapt with the ever-changing landscape of information technology in education. A number of teams work to maintain the campus network, University accounts, hardware and computers, and information security. The Technology Support Center is the first line of support for students, faculty and staff experiencing disruptions with their university account or an issue with a university device. The TSC works to communicate with the other teams in the Office of Technology to ensure issues are escalated, addressed and resolved. Campus Technology Services include oversight of end-user technology resources and maintenance of university devices across all university academic departments and offices. Multimedia Services equips and updates the displays, computers, cameras and other technology that enables students - both in the classroom and online - to better engage with their instructors and course material. Infrastructure Services includes oversight of enterprise and departmental servers and storage, network connectivity, WiFi , and security. Application Systems includes oversight of enterprise and departmental systems to include design, development, implementation, maintenance and support. Technology Operations is responsible for fiscal operations, administrative support, hardware and software contracts, licensing and procurement, and modifying university accounts. Need Tech Support? Accounts, Access, Applications From student workers to the president's office, each university account is managed and updated by the Office of Technology. Student Accounts The Technology Support Center is the first line of support for students who might be experiencing issues with their university account, such as password help, connecting to the wifi and accessing applications like MyCentral and Brightspace. The TSC is available in-person, by phone and email during university operating hours and is located in the lower level of the Ward Edwards Building on the north side of the Quad. New Employees For most university employees, including student workers, account access requests should be made through their supervisor, a department office professional or administrative assistant. Student workers should be made aware that their student and employee accounts are separate. Access to applications and programs for their job are granted through their employee account. Who can contact the TSC? Support is imperative for student success. The TSC works with all UCM offices, departments and students - graduate, undergraduate, and dual credit - to ensure account issues are resolved. The TSC does not discuss sensitive or protected information with anyone except the account holder. Parents and guardians are not permitted to make or request changes to a student's account. Contact the TSC at tsc@ucmo.edu or 660-543-4357. Campus-wide Cybersecurity The Office of Technology Information Security Team maintains protocols and practices to safeguard university systems, services and data from unauthorized access. This includes educating university faculty, staff and students on the importance of cybersecurity. The Information Security Team partners with the greater campus community during October, which is Cybersecurity Awareness Month, to provide information about how to be proactive in protecting personal data and university accounts. Classroom Support Faculty members are encouraged to report interruptions or issues with classroom technology as soon as possible to the TSC at 660-543-4357. Other hardware issues can be reported through the Service Catalog , a resource for faculty and staff members who need to make a request regarding their UCM account, hardware and phones. Student Accounts and Support The Technology Support Center provides general technology support to UCM students for university systems by phone (660-543-4357), email ( tsc@ucmo.edu ), and in-person (WDE 0800). The TSC is available Monday-Friday 7 a.m. to 5 p.m. Students - on campus and online - need to be able to access the appropriate applications and systems to complete their coursework. University systems and applications (such as MyCentral, Brightspace and UCM Email) are accessed through Single Sign-On , and then DUO Multi-Factor Authentication. This two-step process is in place to protect individual accounts and personal data. All UCM students, including dual credit students, are considered account holders. For the safety and security of all university accounts, the TSC cannot discuss or share protected account information with parents, guardians or high school counselors. Dual credit students experiencing account issues (login error, password help, etc) are encouraged to contact their school counselor if they are unable to make a phone call during the normal school day; the student will still need to be present during the call. Office of Technology Accounts/ OT Service Catalog All students and employees of the UCM community are provided an account. This account allows our UCM community to have access to log into their devices, the MyCentral portal, email account, Brightspace, Office365 and other services. Employee Accounts The process for employee account creation and access termination is automated to make the process more effective and efficient, better meeting the needs of the UCM community. How are employee accounts created? Account creation at UCM is generated by input from Human Resources into Banner based on when the employeeâs status as an active employee begins. After an account is created, email notifications are generated and sent to Human Resources, the employeeâs personal email on file, the supervisor of the employee and/or the budget manager of the organization. The email will include the employee's credentials (Network ID and email address). If you receive an email for a new employee that does not report to you or your department, please contact Human Resources. What access is provided with an employee account? Full time employees will automatically receive access to UCM core services of Network ID (NID), email, MyCentral, Brightspace Learn (does not include course assignments), and personal and departmental drive share permissions based on the organization in which the employee is paid. How do I request additional employee access? In the event an employee needs additional accesses and permissions other than those listed, a request will need to be submitted via the OT Service Catalog . How is an employee account access terminated? Termination of access is based on the job end date or termination in Banner. Access and permissions will be removed from an individual's account when that individual separates from UCM or at the end of the position assignment (Emeriti and retirees in good standing excluded). Access to MyCentral will continue after separation for a period of seven years. To ensure timely processing of accounts for departing employees, please notify Human Resources if an employee leaves prior to their expected job end to have an account disabled or accesses removed on the appropriate date (i.e. student workers, temporary workers, etc.). How do I remove access from an employee when they transfer to a new department? If an employee (full or part time, student, temporary, GA) transfers to a new department on campus, you will need to submit a request to remove access to their previous department. How do I request account access for a contractor or vendor? For a contracted, non-instructional, non-UCM employee, please visit our OT Service Catalog to request access. A list of what is needed for this type of account is listed under âAdd a New External Affiliate (XA) Accountâ. For a contracted, instructional, non-UCM employee, please contact the Office of Human Resources. How do I request renewal of an employee account? It is not necessary to request account creation or renewal for any type of employee to include student, graduate assistant, temporary, part time or full time. If an employee is returning to UCM after having been separated for a time, it is not necessary to submit an account renewal request for that employee. Student Accounts How are student accounts created? Student accounts are created after application and admission to University of Central Missouri. Upon admittance to UCM, students will receive an email in their personal account that includes their Banner ID ( also called a 700#) and their Network ID (NID). Upon admission and issuance of an NID, students will be able to log into MyCentral , the university portal. Once a student is enrolled in classes, they will be notified in their personal email account of their UCM email address along with instructions about how to access it. Itâs important for students to use their UCM email address as all future correspondence will be sent to it. What access is provided with a student account? Student accounts will receive a Network ID (NID), Email (Google Workspace), MyCentral, Brightspace Learn, and Office365. How is student account access terminated? Domestic student accounts are deleted after three semesters of non-enrollment. International student accounts are deleted after five years of non-enrollment. For additional assistance or questions, please contact the Technology Support Center by phone at 660.543.4357, via email at tsc@ucmo.edu or in person at WDE 0800. Windows 11 for UCM Computers The Office of Technology is continuing efforts to transition UCM systems running the Windows 10 operating systems to Windows 11. Analyzing the readiness of existing devices for Windows 11 relies solely on the hardware specifications. Once the analysis is conducted, the determination is made whether a device can either be upgraded to Windows 11 or must be replaced. The evaluations are essentially a âpass / failâ exercise. If the device meets the hardware specifications for Windows 11, the device can be upgraded to the Windows 11 operating system. If the device does not meet the hardware specifications for Windows 11, then the device must be replaced before October 2025. Current experiences with devices being upgraded to and running Windows 11 are positive. New systems purchased have the Windows 11 operating system installed. The Office of Technology is now offering Windows 11 to those devices that have passed the hardware specifications test. On the system taskbar, you will see the Fighting Mule Head icon, and if the Fighting Mule Head icon is not present, then left click on the â>â or â^â symbol, which will display the Fighting Mule Head icon. Left click on the Fighting Mule Head icon to open the âUCM Self Serviceâ application. Left click on the âCatalogâ tab to see the offer to upgrade your system to Windows 11. Please know that the Windows 11 upgrade will take some time to complete. The duration of the Windows 11 upgrade is dependent on several factors pertaining to the device characteristics and configuration. Therefore, the Office of Technology recommends that you accept and start the Windows 11 operating system update at the end of the workday. You will need to leave your device powered on, which means laptops should be docked and connected to the power supply. The device will automatically update and restart when the update is finished. The Office of Technology also suggests that everyone in the same immediate office area should not execute the Windows 11 upgrade process at the same time. While the Office of Technology does not anticipate any significant issues, we all know that application upgrades from time to time can create challenges. In addition, the Office of Technology acknowledges that challenges may exist in some areas especially where older software applications and printers are installed. Therefore, the Office of Technology recommends that one or two individuals in each respective area conduct the upgrade to ensure the desired outcome is achieved. If the Windows 11 upgrade on the first few machines goes without incident, then proceed with the upgrade on the other systems in the office. If you have any questions about upgrading to Windows 11 or your encounter any issues with the Windows 11 upgrade process, please call the Technology Support Center at 660-543-4357. You can also email the Technology Support center at tsc@ucmo.edu . Thank you. Multifactor Authentication- MFA Multi-factor authentication provides a second layer of security to any type of login, requiring extra information or a physical device to log in, in addition to your password. Multi-factor authentication can also be called Two-Factor authentication or 2FA. Multi-factor authentication enhances the security of your account by using a secondary device to verify your identity. This prevents anyone but you from accessing your account, even if they know your password. To learn more about how to enroll in Duo or check out the FAQ's about MFA , please visit our Service Catalog Knowledge Base. UCM Single Sign-On Single Sign-On (SSO) is a capability that allows you to: Log into UCM software or web applications with your Network ID and password (i.e. credentials) Access other applications without entering your credentials again. Without SSO, you encounter different logon screens and need to use different credentials to access specific applications. With SSO, you can experience a common logon screen and use the same credentials, if prompted at all. For more detailed information, please visit this page .
